title: Equipment Technician, Vacuum, Semiconductor, Raxium
description: Perform routine preventative and corrective maintenance to maximize equipment uptime. Perform repairs and modifications on production tools. Execute electro-mechanical and pneumatic troubleshooting on a variety of toolsets. Collaborate with process and equipment engineering to improve tool performance and reliability. Support the equipment engineering department with maintenance, tool installations, retrofits, and repairs as needed. Minimum qualifications: Associate's degree with a focus in Electrical Engineering, or a related engineering field, or equivalent practical experience. 2 years of experience maintaining and troubleshooting semiconductor processing equipment (vacuum systems, wet etch, lithography or chemical mechanical planarization (CMP) tools). Experience in reading blueprints, mechanical drawings, and electrical schematics. Experience troubleshooting equipment by checking software logs, programmable logic controller (PLC) signals, and wiring to find the root cause of a failure. Preferred qualifications: 2 years of experience with development of manufacturing processes including acceptance testing, startup, and qualification. 1 year of experience creating and maintaining technical documentation (drawings, schematics, standard operating procedure, etc.). 1 year of experience in technical leadership with an ability to search with original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to understand hardware roadmaps. Experience supporting emergency response and safety teams. Knowledge of preventative maintenance systems, gas meters and calibration, disaster plans, laser safety, and x-ray equipment inspection and badge programs. Excellent sense of ownership with the ability to be detail-oriented and organized. Google's custom-designed machines make up one of the largest and most powerful computing infrastructures in the world. The Hardware Testing Engineering team ensures that this cutting-edge equipment is reliable. In the R&D lab, you design test equipment for prototypes

title: Graduate 2026 PhD Scientist II (AEA/ASSA Economists Only), Reservations
description: **About the Role** The Reserve product is a maturing product, most of the “low hanging fruit” initiatives to improve the experience to consistently deliver high reliability have been delivered. Currently, our focus is in building smart levers to maintain reliability while driving efficiency, improving our unit economics and growing adoption and retention. Some of the key projects involve major changes in Pricing, Matching and Dispatch that involve transitioning from heuristic based decision making to building machine learning models to make key decisions in real time, as well as important changes to Driver and Rider experience, including discoverability and awareness generation levers, and finally piloting new use cases that leverage the Reserve Tech as a platform to unlock new vectors of growth. **What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points** **What the Candidate Will Do** 1. Work together with Product, Operations, and Engineering partners to design a roadmap of features and initiatives as well as the long-term team strategy 2. Run large scale experiments, observational studies, deep dives into business and user behavior trends and present findings to technical and non-technical audiences **Basic Qualifications** 1. Ph.D., M.S. or Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Machine Learning, Operations Research, or other quantitative fields. 2. 2+ years of industry experience as an Applied or Data Scientist or equivalent (not required with Ph.D.). 3. Proficiency in programming languages (Python, Java, Scala) and ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-Learn), underpinned by a solid grasp of MLOps practices, including design documentation, testing, and source code management with Git. 4. Good understanding of experimental design and analysis (e.g., A/B and market-level experiments), causal inference. 5. Good business and product sense: delight in shaping vague questions into well-defined analyses and success metrics that drive business decisio
